title: With 2FA enabled, podman login to Docker Hub throws incorrect credential error instead of asking for access token
body:
### Issue Description

If you have 2FA enabled on Docker Hub, you need an access token for login.

https://hub.docker.com/settings/security

`podman login` doesn't state this, but instead, says your credentials are incorrect.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman login docker.io
```

### Describe the results you received

```
Username: bviktor
Password:
Error: error logging into "docker.io": invalid username/password
```

### Describe the results you expected

This is how `docker` deals with this:

```
$ docker login docker.io
Login with your Docker ID to push and pull images from Docker Hub. If you don't have a Docker ID, head over to https://hub.docker.com to create one.
Username: bviktor
Password:
Error response from daemon: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": unauthorized: please use personal access token to login
```

So surely, there must be a way to detect this scenario. After using the access token with `podman login`, everything works fine.

### podman info output

_No response_

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @bviktor!

I see the error in the debug logs:
> DEBU[0004] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401 
DEBU[0004] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=valentinrothberg&service=registry.docker.io 
DEBU[0004] error logging into "docker.io": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized: please use personal access token to login

@mtrmac, should we special case this error?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg @mtrmac a way forward on this one?
Just need to get someone to step up and do the work.

> Just need to get someone to step up and do the work.

I will take a look.

https://github.com/containers/image/blob/07ced61370833cfc8bf17549c4a0121d7abb5a59/docker/errors.go#L25-L27

@mtrmac, I wonder whether we shouldn't directly change `func (e ErrUnauthorizedForCredentials) Error()`?  It would feel strange to first add `unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password:` and then strip it off in libimage.  Maybe I am misreading your comment above.

`docker.ErrUnauthorizedForCredentials` results in a long error message, but it’s not a cause of the primary problem reported in this issue, namely that the server-provided message about tokens is completely thrown away. (We also can’t completely get rid of it because it’s an API, used by `c/common/pkg/auth` for starters.)

I agree that tuning the error message there could make things better.

---

The _primary_ issue that needs fixing, though, is the c/common/pkg/auth path linked above, which wraps `docker.ErrUnauthorizedForCredentials` in `auth.ErrNewCredentialsInvalid` in a way that completely discards the server-provided message.

That must be fixed first; then we can discuss what the error message formed by the combination of the two wrappers should be.

Quite possibly the two wrappers should not be adding any context if the failure uses an unexpected message. Compare https://github.com/containers/image/blob/07ced61370833cfc8bf17549c4a0121d7abb5a59/docker/errors.go#L90 .
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Hello,

I just moved to Podman from Docker on macOS. It is really annoying nowadays if it is not possible to use tokens to connect to registries. If a solution is found, it is not apparent from the documentation.
Eventually, I found this thread that has been open for more than two years.
What is the solution? How are you guys dealing with this issue?

I see a lot of discussion. Why don't you add a toggle on the configure screen labeled "Add Registry" to allow for a token instead?

UPDATE:

I apologize, I found the item in the documentation eventually. I was looking for the bad reference. **In the password field, a token may be used**. Again, sorry for the post.
It might help some people, though.

Cheers,
Jean

> Why don't you add a toggle on the configure screen labeled "Add Registry"

This Podman project does not have “screens”. Did you mean this as a suggestion for https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop ?

Hello,

Yes, indeed. 
I came to this thread looking for a solution. 
Sorry for the intrusion.

Regards,
Jean
